source: runtime dispatch miss log â€” functions called indirectly (JALR / function pointer tables)
note: These functions have no static JAL callers and cannot be found by transitive discovery. They are reached at runtime via BIOS API dispatch tables (A0/B0/C0 vectors) or other indirect call mechanisms.

dispatch miss: KP2 secondary entry point for exception handler, mid-function split of func_00001A00, called during VBlank
dispatch miss: KP2 function, tail-call target from 0xBFC11524 exception handler path
dispatch miss (Beetle wtrace, docs/CASE_A_AOT_GAP.md): chain BUSY-writer at RAM 0x4F54; alias rule RAM=ROM-0xBFC10000+0x500. Reached via JAL inside FUN_bfc147f4; never emitted as standalone entry.
dispatch miss (Beetle wtrace, docs/CASE_A_AOT_GAP.md): chain init slot-0 BUSY writer at RAM 0x5DD0; alias RAM=ROM-0xBFC10000+0x500. Inside FUN_bfc158a8 body.
dispatch miss (Beetle wtrace, docs/CASE_A_AOT_GAP.md): chain init slot-1 BUSY writer at RAM 0x5DD8; alias RAM=ROM-0xBFC10000+0x500. Inside FUN_bfc158a8 body.

dispatch miss (Beetle wtrace, docs/CASE_A_AOT_GAP.md): chain step handler that writes 0x02 OPEN to [0x7568+slot] at RAM 0x5EF4. Indirect-jr target from RAM 0x6c98 jump table; alias RAM=ROM-0xBFC10000+0x500. KEY entry â€” without this the gate never clears.

dispatch miss (Beetle wtrace, docs/CASE_A_AOT_GAP.md): chain step handler that writes 0x04 OPEN to [0x7568+slot] at RAM 0x5FA8. Indirect-jr target from RAM 0x6c98 jump table; alias RAM=ROM-0xBFC10000+0x500. KEY entry â€” paired with 0xBFC159F4.

Tomba boot dispatch miss after licensed PlayStation screen. Verified in Ghidra/runtime disassembly as a standalone BIOS init routine between 0xBFC048D0 and 0xBFC04ABC, with return at 0xBFC04A98 and table data reference from 0xBFC04554.

Tomba boot dispatch miss after seeding 0xBFC04910. Verified in Ghidra/runtime disassembly as a standalone BIOS callback-list init routine between 0xBFC0472C and 0xBFC048D0, with return at 0xBFC048C8 and table data reference from 0xBFC04588.
Tomba boot dispatch miss after game callback coverage fixes. Verified in Ghidra as a tiny BIOS table leaf routine (sw a0,0(sp); jr ra; clear v0) referenced by data at 0xBFC04484 and 0xBFC0E30C.

dirty_ram hot: SIO/PAD interrupt handler entry point (RAM 0x641C), called via jalr. 10852+ hits/frame across internal branch targets (0x641C, 0x6430, 0x6444, 0x6458, 0x650C, 0x6514, 0x658C, 0x6594). Seeding entry only; CFG discovers internal labels.

kernel jump-table target: A0:0F (unresolved (see psx-spx A0 table)) — ROM-resident entry of the post-init A0/B0/C0 tables (read from live kernel RAM 0x200/0x874/0x674); reachable only through the vector table, invisible to transitive discovery. Class found via MMX6 card-check: A0:2B memset dispatch-missed silently, killing the save-scan (2026-07-02).
